title: Baked Spinach Feta Egg Frittata
ingredients: 1 cup fresh baby spinach, roughly chopped
1/4 cup kalamata olives, pitted and sliced
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe 10-inch skillet over medium heat and sauté onion for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute more until fragrant.
Add spinach and stir for 2 minutes until wilted.
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, and oregano in a bowl, then pour over the vegetables.
Scatter cherry tomatoes, feta, and olives evenly over the top.
Cook undisturbed on the stovetop for 2 minutes until edges begin to set.
Transfer skillet to the oven and bake 12-15 minutes until the center is firm and the top is lightly golden.
Let rest 5 minutes before slicing into wedges and serving.

title: Overnight Mango Coconut Chia Pudding
ingredients: 1/2 cup fresh or frozen mango chunks
2 tbsp unsweetened shredded coconut, for topping
steps: Whisk chia seeds, coconut milk, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and lime juice together in a jar or bowl.
Stir vigorously, then let sit 5 minutes and stir again to prevent clumping.
Cover and refrigerate overnight or at least 6 hours until thick and pudding-like.
Blend mango chunks with lime zest in a small blender until smooth to form a mango coulis.
Spoon chia pudding into serving glasses, pour mango coulis over the top, and garnish with shredded coconut and raspberries.
Serve cold straight from the refrigerator.

title: Spiced Tomato Pepper Shakshuka
steps: Heat olive oil in a large deep skillet over medium heat and sauté onion and bell pepper for 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes, season with salt and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Simmer sauce uncovered for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
Use a spoon to make 6 shallow wells in the sauce and crack one egg into each well.
Cover the pan and cook on medium-low heat for 8-10 minutes until whites are fully set but yolks remain slightly soft.
Scatter fresh parsley over the top and serve directly from the pan.

title: Grilled Lemon Herb Chicken Bowl
ingredients: 10 oz boneless skinless chicken thighs (2 thighs)
1 medium sweet potato, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes
1 lemon, cut into wedges for serving
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and toss sweet potato cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, paprika, a pinch of salt, and pepper on a baking sheet.
Roast sweet potato for 22-25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until tender and caramelized at the edges.
Pound chicken thighs to an even 3/4-inch thickness and marinate in lemon juice, remaining olive oil, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper for at least 10 minutes.
Heat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat and grill chicken 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Rest chicken 5 minutes, then slice against the grain.
Divide baby spinach between two bowls and arrange roasted sweet potato, sliced chicken, and avocado over the top.
Serve with lemon wedges for squeezing over the bowl.

title: Almond Flour Blueberry Pancakes
ingredients: extra blueberries and maple syrup for serving
steps: Whisk eggs, almond milk, maple syrup, and vanilla extract together in a large mixing bowl.
Add almond flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt and stir until a smooth batter forms; let rest 2 minutes.
Gently fold in blueberries without crushing them.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-low heat and melt coconut oil.
Pour approximately 1/4 cup of batter per pancake and cook 3-4 minutes until bubbles form on the surface and edges look dry and set.
Flip carefully with a wide spatula and cook 2-3 minutes more until lightly golden on the bottom.
Serve stacked with fresh blueberries and a drizzle of maple syrup.

title: Savory Miso Mushroom Rice Porridge
ingredients: 1 cup shiitake mushrooms, stems removed and caps sliced
1 sheet nori, cut into thin strips
steps: Rinse rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then combine with vegetable broth in a medium saucepan.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a low simmer and cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 30-35 minutes until rice breaks down into a creamy porridge.
While rice cooks, heat sesame oil in a small skillet over medium heat and sauté shiitake mushrooms with garlic and ginger for 5-6 minutes until tender and lightly golden.
Stir tamari into the mushrooms and remove from heat.
Dissolve miso paste in 2 tbsp of warm water and stir into the finished porridge off the heat.
Ladle porridge into bowls and top with sautéed mushrooms, scallions, nori strips, and sesame seeds.
Serve hot immediately.

title: Tropical Green Smoothie Bowl
steps: Combine frozen mango, frozen banana, spinach, coconut milk, and lime juice in a high-speed blender.
Blend on high until completely smooth and very thick, stopping to scrape down the sides as needed; add 1-2 tbsp more coconut milk only if mixture is too thick to blend.
Pour into two wide, shallow bowls and spread evenly with the back of a spoon.
Arrange pineapple chunks, kiwi slices, chia seeds, hemp seeds, and coconut flakes decoratively over the top.
Serve immediately before the bowl thaws and becomes liquid.

title: Crispy Bacon Avocado Egg Scramble
steps: Cook bacon in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat for 6-8 minutes until crispy, then transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain and crumble when cool.
Discard all but 1 tsp of bacon fat from the pan, add olive oil, and reduce heat to medium-low.
Add spinach to the pan and stir for 1 minute until just wilted.
Whisk eggs with garlic powder, salt, and black pepper, then pour over the wilted spinach.
Use a silicone spatula to gently pull the eggs from the edges toward the center in large slow folds, cooking 3-4 minutes total.
Remove from heat while eggs are still slightly glossy and just set to prevent overcooking.
Fold in cherry tomatoes, diced avocado, and crumbled bacon gently.
Garnish with fresh chives and serve immediately.

title: Smoked Salmon Avocado Breakfast Plate
ingredients: 1/2 English cucumber, sliced into thin rounds
1/4 small red onion, very thinly sliced into rings
steps: Arrange cucumber rounds in an overlapping ring on two serving plates.
Drape smoked salmon slices over the cucumber in loose folds.
Fan avocado slices alongside the salmon.
Scatter red onion rings and capers evenly over the plate.
Drizzle lemon juice and olive oil over all components.
Sprinkle fresh dill, black pepper, and sesame seeds over the top.
Serve immediately at room temperature.

title: Sweet Potato Black Bean Breakfast Tacos
ingredients: 1 medium sweet potato, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes
1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and add diced sweet potato in a single layer.
Cook sweet potato, stirring every 2-3 minutes, for 10-12 minutes until golden on the outside and tender throughout.
Stir in black beans, cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, and salt and cook 3-4 minutes until beans are heated through and coated in spices.
Warm corn tortillas directly over a gas flame for 20-30 seconds per side until pliable with light char marks, or in a dry skillet over medium heat.
Spoon the sweet potato and black bean mixture into each tortilla.
Top with avocado slices, a spoonful of fresh salsa, and a scatter of cilantro.
Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over the top.

title: Savory Herb Chickpea Socca
steps: Whisk chickpea flour, warm water, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, black pepper, and cumin together in a bowl until completely smooth; let batter rest for 20 minutes.
Heat remaining 1 tbsp olive oil in a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Pour half the batter into the pan, tilting to spread into an even round; cook undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes until the edges are set and the bottom is golden brown.
Flip carefully and cook 2 more minutes; slide onto a plate and repeat with remaining batter.
Return the pan to medium heat, add cherry tomatoes and garlic, and cook for 3 minutes, stirring, until tomatoes soften and burst.
Spoon the tomato mixture over each socca, scatter fresh basil and red pepper flakes on top, and serve warm.

title: Baked Chorizo Egg Pepper Cups
ingredients: 3 large bell peppers, halved lengthwise and seeded
6 oz fresh Mexican-style chorizo, casing removed
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and arrange the bell pepper halves cut-side up in a lightly oiled baking dish.
Cook chorizo, onion, and garlic in a skillet over medium heat, breaking up the meat, for 5 to 6 minutes until browned; drain any excess fat.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper and cook 1 minute more.
Divide the chorizo mixture evenly among the pepper halves, pressing it into a nest with a slight well in the center.
Crack one egg directly into the well of each pepper half.
Bake for 18 to 22 minutes until egg whites are fully set and yolks are cooked to your preference.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve alongside sliced avocado.

title: Fluffy Lemon Ricotta Hotcakes
ingredients: 2 tbsp unsalted butter, for the pan
steps: Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar together in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, stir ricotta, egg yolks, milk, lemon juice, lemon zest, and vanilla until smooth.
Beat egg whites in a clean bowl with a hand mixer until stiff peaks form.
Fold the ricotta mixture into the flour mixture until just combined, then gently fold in the beaten egg whites in two additions, keeping the batter light.
Melt 1/2 tbsp butter on a nonstick griddle or skillet over medium-low heat.
Scoop 1/4 cup batter per pancake; cook 3 to 4 minutes until bubbles form across the surface and edges look set, then flip and cook 2 more minutes until golden.
Serve three hotcakes per portion topped with fresh blueberries and a drizzle of maple syrup.

title: Paleo Sweet Potato Sausage Hash
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch dice
8 oz ground pork breakfast sausage (no fillers or added sugar)
steps: Heat 1 tbsp coconut oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat; add sweet potato in a single layer and cook, stirring every 2 minutes, for 8 to 10 minutes until golden on the outside and just tender.
Push sweet potato to the edge of the pan, add sausage to the center, and cook for 5 minutes, breaking it apart, until browned through.
Add remaining 1 tbsp coconut oil, bell pepper, and onion; stir everything together and cook 3 to 4 minutes until vegetables soften.
Stir in garlic, smoked paprika, sage, salt, and pepper; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add spinach and toss until fully wilted, about 1 minute.
Use a spoon to create 4 evenly spaced wells in the hash; crack one egg into each well and cover the skillet with a lid.
Cook 4 to 5 minutes until egg whites are just set; serve directly from the skillet.

title: Japanese Rolled Egg Tamagoyaki
ingredients: 2 tbsp dashi stock or low-sodium vegetable broth
1 tbsp neutral vegetable oil, for the pan
steps: Whisk eggs, dashi stock, tamari, mirin, sugar, and sesame oil together in a bowl until the mixture is completely uniform with no streaks of white.
Heat a tamagoyaki pan or a small nonstick skillet over medium heat and brush lightly with vegetable oil.
Pour one-third of the egg mixture into the pan, tilting to coat evenly; scatter half the green onions over the surface.

When the egg is just set but still slightly moist on top, use chopsticks or a flexible spatula to roll the egg log from the far end toward you into a tight cylinder; push it to the far side of the pan.
Brush exposed pan with a little more oil, pour in another third of the egg mixture, and lift the existing roll so fresh egg flows underneath it.
Roll again from the far end toward you to incorporate the new layer; repeat once more with the remaining egg mixture and green onions.
Remove from heat, place the roll on a cutting board, and rest for 2 minutes before slicing into 1-inch rounds.
Arrange slices on a plate, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and serve with pickled ginger alongside.

title: Bright Greek Chickpea Feta Salad
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
steps: Drain and rinse the chickpeas, then spread them on a paper towel and pat dry
Combine chickpeas, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and olives in a large mixing bowl
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper in a small bowl until combined
Pour dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat all ingredients
Fold in crumbled feta and fresh parsley
Let the salad rest at room temperature for 10 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld

title: Sautéed Turkey Asian Lettuce Cups
ingredients: 1 can (8 oz) water chestnuts, drained and finely chopped
steps: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering
Add garlic and ginger and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant
Add ground turkey and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, for 6 to 8 minutes until fully browned
Stir in coconut aminos, rice vinegar, and red pepper flakes and cook 2 more minutes
Remove skillet from heat and stir in sesame oil, lime juice, and shredded carrots
Fold in water chestnuts and two-thirds of the sliced green onions
Spoon filling evenly into lettuce cups and garnish with remaining green onions

title: Roasted Veggie Black Bean Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
2 bell peppers, any color, sliced into strips
1 medium red onion, sliced into wedges
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper
Toss bell peppers, zucchini, red onion, and corn with 2 tbsp olive oil, cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper
Spread vegetables in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet and roast 22 to 25 minutes until tender and lightly charred at edges
Warm black beans in a small saucepan with remaining 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat for 3 minutes and season with salt
Divide cooked brown rice among 4 bowls
Top each bowl with roasted vegetables, seasoned black beans, and avocado slices
Drizzle with lime juice and scatter fresh cilantro over the top before serving

title: Chilled Spanish Summer Tomato Gazpacho
ingredients: 2 lbs ripe Roma tomatoes, roughly chopped
1 large red bell pepper, seeded and chopped
steps: Combine tomatoes, red bell pepper, cucumber, red onion, and garlic in a high-powered blender
Blend on high for 1 minute until very smooth
With the blender running on low, slowly drizzle in olive oil and sherry vinegar
Add cumin, salt, pepper, and cold water and blend another 30 seconds
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or vinegar as needed
Transfer to a sealed container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until thoroughly chilled
Serve in bowls garnished with diced cucumber and fresh basil leaves

title: Spiced Lamb Cauliflower Stuffed Peppers
ingredients: 6 large bell peppers, tops cut off and seeded
2 cups cauliflower rice, fresh or thawed from frozen
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and arrange the hollowed peppers upright in a deep baking dish
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat; add onion and cook 4 minutes until softened
Add garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and paprika and stir 1 minute until fragrant
Add ground lamb and cook 6 to 7 minutes, breaking apart, until browned; carefully drain excess fat
Stir in cauliflower rice and drained diced tomatoes and cook 3 minutes until cauliflower is just tender
Remove skillet from heat; season filling with salt and pepper and stir in fresh mint and pine nuts
Spoon filling generously into each pepper, pressing gently to pack
Cover dish tightly with foil and bake 30 minutes; remove foil and bake an additional 10 minutes until peppers are tender

title: Avocado Dijon Shredded Chicken Salad
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
1 head butter lettuce, leaves separated for serving
steps: Place chicken breasts in a saucepan and cover with cold water; bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat
Cook chicken 15 to 17 minutes until internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C); remove and let cool completely
Shred cooled chicken using two forks into bite-sized pieces
Whisk together lemon juice, Dijon mustard, olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a large bowl
Add shredded chicken, celery, and red onion to the bowl and toss to coat in dressing
Gently fold in avocado cubes, fresh dill, and fresh parsley, being careful not to mash the avocado
Serve over butter lettuce leaves

title: Creamy Coconut Spiced Red Lentil Dal
steps: Heat coconut oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat; add onion and cook 5 minutes until golden
Add garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, and garam masala and stir constantly for 1 minute until very fragrant
Pour in coconut milk, diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth; stir well to combine
Add rinsed red lentils and bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 20 to 22 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are completely soft and creamy
Stir in fresh spinach and cook 2 minutes until wilted
Add lime juice and season generously with salt
Serve topped with fresh cilantro

title: Cold Thai Peanut Rice Noodle Salad
steps: Cook rice noodles according to package directions; drain and rinse thoroughly under cold running water
Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, maple syrup, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes together in a large bowl until smooth
If dressing is too thick, whisk in 2 to 3 tbsp warm water until pourable
Add cooled noodles to the bowl and toss thoroughly to coat every strand in peanut dressing
Add cucumber, carrots, edamame, and two-thirds of the green onions; toss again to distribute evenly
Transfer to serving bowls and garnish with remaining green onions, sesame seeds, and lime wedges

title: Pan-Seared Tuna Niçoise Salad
ingredients: 2 fresh tuna steaks, about 6 oz each
3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil for dressing
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Boil potatoes in a pot of salted water for 12 to 14 minutes until just fork-tender; drain and allow to cool
Blanch green beans in boiling salted water for 3 minutes; immediately transfer to a bowl of ice water, then drain
Whisk together 3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, shallot, salt, and pepper in a small bowl to make dressing
Pat tuna steaks completely dry with paper towels and season generously on both sides with salt and pepper
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a heavy skillet over high heat until just smoking; sear tuna 1.5 to 2 minutes per side for medium-rare
Transfer tuna to a cutting board, rest 2 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain
Arrange salad greens, potatoes, green beans, tomatoes, olives, capers, and halved eggs on a large platter
Lay sliced tuna over the top, drizzle with dressing, and garnish with fresh thyme leaves

title: Baked Tomato Mozzarella Portobello Caps
ingredients: 4 large portobello mushroom caps, stems and gills removed
6 oz fresh mozzarella, sliced into rounds
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C) and line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper
Brush both sides of each portobello cap with 2 tbsp olive oil; arrange gill-side up on the baking sheet and season with salt and pepper
Roast mushrooms for 10 minutes; carefully tilt each cap and blot away any released liquid with a paper towel
Combine cherry tomatoes, garlic, remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, dried oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper in a small bowl
Spoon tomato mixture evenly into each mushroom cap
Layer fresh mozzarella slices over the tomato filling in each cap
Bake 12 to 14 minutes until mozzarella is fully melted and beginning to bubble
Drizzle balsamic vinegar over each cap and scatter torn basil leaves on top before serving

title: Grilled Herb Shrimp Quinoa Bowl
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Rinse quinoa under cold water, then combine with broth in a saucepan and bring to a boil; reduce heat, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed; fluff with a fork and set aside to cool slightly.
Toss shrimp with 1 tbsp olive oil, half the minced garlic, oregano, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Preheat a grill pan or outdoor grill over medium-high heat and grill shrimp 2–3 minutes per side until pink and opaque; remove from heat.
Whisk together remaining 2 tbsp olive oil, remaining garlic, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a small bowl to make the dressing.
Toss cooked quinoa with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and red onion; drizzle with dressing and stir to combine.
Divide quinoa mixture among four bowls, top evenly with grilled shrimp, and garnish with fresh parsley.

title: Smoky Roasted Red Pepper Bisque
ingredients: 1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes, no salt added
salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F and line a large baking sheet with foil; arrange whole peppers, onion quarters, and unpeeled garlic cloves on the sheet and drizzle with 2 tbsp olive oil.
Roast for 30–35 minutes, turning peppers once halfway, until skins are charred and blistered.
Transfer peppers to a bowl, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and let steam 10 minutes; peel off skins, remove stems and seeds, and roughly chop.
Squeeze roasted garlic from its skins and discard the skins.
Combine roasted peppers, onion, garlic, diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, smoked paprika, cumin, and cayenne in a blender; blend on high until completely smooth.
Pour soup into a medium saucepan over medium heat, stir in balsamic vinegar and remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, and simmer 10 minutes; season with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and top with torn fresh basil before serving.

title: Spiced Ground Beef Lettuce Cups
ingredients: salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat; add onion and cook, stirring, 3–4 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add garlic and jalapeño and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add ground beef, breaking it apart with a spoon, and cook 6–7 minutes until browned and cooked through; carefully drain excess fat.
Stir in cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, cayenne, and tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Pour in beef broth and simmer 3–4 minutes, stirring, until liquid reduces and mixture is lightly saucy; season with salt and pepper.
Toss diced avocado gently with lime juice in a small bowl to prevent browning.
Spoon beef mixture into lettuce cups and top each with diced avocado and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro.

title: Tuscan White Bean Kale Soup
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
1 bunch Tuscan kale (about 8 oz), stems removed and leaves chopped
salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat olive oil in a large heavy pot over medium heat; add onion, carrots, and celery and cook, stirring occasionally, 5–6 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, rosemary, thyme, and red pepper flakes and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in diced tomatoes and vegetable broth and bring to a boil; add one can of cannellini beans.
Place the second can of beans in a bowl and mash roughly with a fork until pasty; stir into the pot to thicken the soup.
Reduce heat to medium-low, stir in chopped kale, and simmer 8–10 minutes until kale is tender.
Stir in lemon juice, season with salt and pepper, and serve hot.

title: Cold Sesame Ginger Chicken Soba
ingredients: 2 tbsp water, or more to thin dressing
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil; poach chicken breast over medium heat 15–18 minutes until cooked through (internal temperature 165°F), then remove and let cool before shredding into bite-sized pieces.
Cook soba noodles in boiling water according to package directions; drain and rinse immediately under cold running water until fully cooled.
Whisk together tahini, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, ginger, garlic, and honey in a bowl until smooth; add water one tablespoon at a time until dressing is pourable.
Toss chilled soba noodles with half the dressing until evenly coated.
Divide noodles among four bowls; top each with shredded chicken, julienned cucumber, and julienned carrots.
Drizzle remaining dressing over each bowl and garnish with green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and red pepper flakes.

title: Moroccan Lamb and Apricot Tagine
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Season lamb pieces generously with salt, pepper, cinnamon, cumin, ginger, and turmeric
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown lamb on all sides, about 3 minutes per side, then remove and set aside
Sauté onion in the same pot until softened, about 5 minutes, then add garlic and cook 1 minute until fragrant
Add cayenne, diced tomatoes, chicken broth, apricots, olives, and honey, stirring to combine
Return lamb to pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 1.5 hours until lamb is fork-tender
Uncover and simmer an additional 10 minutes to thicken the sauce
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper
Ladle into bowls and garnish generously with fresh cilantro before serving

title: Creamy Spinach Gnocchi Skillet
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Cook gnocchi according to package directions until they float, then drain and set aside
Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat and sauté garlic and red pepper flakes for 1 minute until fragrant
Pour in heavy cream and vegetable broth, stir to combine, and bring to a gentle simmer
Cook sauce over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened
Add drained gnocchi to the sauce and toss gently to coat
Stir in baby spinach and cook 2 minutes until just wilted
Remove from heat and fold in Parmesan and ground nutmeg
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately topped with torn basil

title: Korean Bulgogi Beef Rice Bowl
steps: Whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and grated pear in a large bowl
Add sliced beef and toss thoroughly to coat, then cover and marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat until almost smoking
Spread beef in a single layer and cook undisturbed for 2 minutes to develop caramelization, then stir-fry for another 2 minutes
Divide cooked rice into serving bowls and arrange shredded napa cabbage alongside
Spoon caramelized bulgogi beef over the rice and cabbage
Garnish with sliced green onions, sesame seeds, and a small dollop of gochujang

title: Mediterranean Herb Baked Halibut
ingredients: 1/4 cup kalamata olives, pitted and halved
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C)
Pat halibut fillets dry with paper towels and season on both sides with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika
Whisk together olive oil, garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, parsley, and oregano in a small bowl
Place fillets in a single layer in a baking dish and spoon herb oil evenly over them
Scatter cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, and capers around and on top of the fish
Bake for 14 to 16 minutes until fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork
Spoon the pan juices over the fish and serve immediately

title: Zucchini Noodles with Walnut Pesto
ingredients: 1/4 cup olive oil, plus 1 tbsp for sautéing
steps: Combine basil, walnuts, garlic, nutritional yeast, 1/4 cup olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a food processor and blend until smooth, scraping down sides as needed
Spiralize zucchini into noodles using a spiralizer; pat lightly with paper towels to remove excess moisture
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
Add zucchini noodles and sauté for 2 to 3 minutes until just tender but still slightly firm, seasoning lightly with salt
Remove pan from heat and immediately toss noodles with walnut pesto until evenly coated
Top with halved cherry tomatoes and a pinch of red pepper flakes
Serve right away to prevent the noodles from releasing excess water

title: Smoky Black Bean Enchilada Bake
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) black beans, drained and rinsed
1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes with green chiles
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C)
Mix black beans, diced tomatoes with chiles, corn, cumin, smoked paprika, and garlic powder together in a large bowl
Spread 1/2 cup enchilada sauce evenly over the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish
Arrange 4 corn tortillas over the sauce, overlapping slightly to cover the bottom
Spread half the bean mixture over the tortillas and pour 3/4 cup enchilada sauce on top
Sprinkle half the cheese, then layer remaining tortillas, bean mixture, and remaining enchilada sauce
Top with remaining cheese and bake uncovered for 25 to 30 minutes until cheese is bubbly and edges are set
Let the casserole rest 5 minutes, then serve with sour cream, fresh cilantro, jalapeño slices, and lime wedges

title: Lemon Dill Chicken Orzo Soup
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and sauté onion, celery, and carrots until softened, about 6 minutes
Add garlic and cook 1 minute more until fragrant
Add chicken thighs, chicken broth, oregano, thyme, lemon zest, salt, and pepper
Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until chicken is cooked through
Remove chicken to a cutting board and shred with two forks, then return shredded meat to the pot
Stir in orzo and cook uncovered over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until pasta is tender
Add lemon juice and baby spinach, stirring until spinach wilts, about 1 minute
Ladle into bowls and top generously with fresh dill before serving

title: Thai Basil Tofu Stir Fry
ingredients: 14 oz extra-firm tofu, pressed and cut into 1-inch cubes
1 cup green beans, trimmed and halved
steps: Press tofu dry between paper towels for at least 15 minutes, then cut into 1-inch cubes
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat and fry tofu cubes until golden on all sides, about 8 minutes total, then remove and set aside
Add remaining oil to the wok and stir-fry garlic and Thai chilies for 30 seconds until aromatic
Add red bell pepper and green beans and stir-fry over high heat for 3 to 4 minutes until crisp-tender
Return tofu to the wok and add soy sauce, vegan oyster sauce, sesame oil, and sugar
Toss everything together over high heat for 1 minute until sauce coats all ingredients
Remove from heat and fold in fresh basil leaves until just wilted
Serve over jasmine rice with lime wedges on the side

title: Smoky Paprika Roasted Chicken Thighs
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C)
Pat chicken thighs completely dry with paper towels to ensure crispy skin
Combine smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, cayenne, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl
Rub chicken thighs all over with olive oil then coat evenly with the spice mixture, working some under the skin
Arrange chicken thighs skin-side up in a large oven-safe skillet or baking dish with lemon slices and smashed garlic tucked around them
Roast for 35 to 40 minutes until skin is deeply golden and crispy and internal temperature reaches 165°F on an instant-read thermometer
Let chicken rest 5 minutes before serving, garnished with fresh parsley

title: Pan-Seared Scallops Cauliflower Puree
ingredients: 1.5 lbs large sea scallops (about 16 to 18 scallops)
1 large head cauliflower, cut into florets
Salt and white pepper to taste
steps: Boil cauliflower florets in salted water until very tender, about 12 minutes, then drain thoroughly
Transfer cauliflower to a blender with 2 tbsp butter, heavy cream, garlic, nutmeg, salt, and white pepper and blend until silky smooth, adjusting consistency with chicken broth as needed
Pat scallops completely dry with paper towels on all sides and season with salt and white pepper
Heat olive oil in a stainless steel or cast-iron skillet over high heat until just smoking
Place scallops in the pan without crowding and sear undisturbed for 2 minutes until a deep golden crust forms, then flip and cook 1 to 2 minutes more
Transfer scallops to a plate, reduce heat to medium, and add remaining butter, lemon juice, and capers to pan, swirling to form a quick pan sauce
Spoon warm cauliflower puree onto plates, arrange seared scallops on top, drizzle with pan sauce, and garnish with fresh chives

title: Ethiopian Berbere Spiced Red Lentil Stew
steps: Heat coconut oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add onion and cook 6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent.
Stir in garlic, ginger, berbere, and turmeric; cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add lentils, diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth; stir to combine and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover partially, and simmer 25 minutes until lentils are completely tender.
Stir in lemon juice and season with salt to taste.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Seared Garlic Herb Ribeye with Mushrooms
steps: Remove steaks from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking and pat completely dry with paper towels.
Season both sides of each steak generously with sea salt and cracked black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a cast-iron skillet over high heat until just smoking.
Sear steaks 3 to 4 minutes per side without moving for a deep crust.
Reduce heat to medium and add butter, garlic, thyme, and rosemary to the skillet.
Tilt the pan and baste steaks repeatedly with the foamy herb butter for 1 minute.
Transfer steaks to a cutting board and rest 5 minutes.
Add mushrooms to the same skillet and sauté over medium-high heat 5 minutes until golden.
Slice steaks and serve topped with mushrooms and pan juices, garnished with parsley.

title: Baked Cod with Olive and Caper Tapenade
ingredients: 1/2 cup pitted Kalamata olives, finely chopped
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and lightly coat a baking dish with olive oil.
Combine olives, capers, garlic, 2 tablespoons olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, parsley, oregano, and red pepper flakes in a small bowl.
Pat cod fillets dry and season both sides with salt and black pepper.
Arrange fillets in the prepared baking dish and spoon tapenade evenly over the top of each.
Drizzle remaining tablespoon of olive oil over the fish.
Bake 15 to 18 minutes until fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Serve immediately with lemon slices alongside.

title: Thai Green Curry Shrimp Bowl
ingredients: 1.5 lbs large shrimp, peeled and deveined
2 cans (13.5 oz each) full-fat coconut milk
steps: Cook jasmine rice according to package directions and keep warm.
Heat coconut oil in a large wok or deep skillet over medium-high heat.
Add green curry paste and cook 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Pour in both cans of coconut milk and stir until paste is fully incorporated.
Add kaffir lime leaves, fish sauce, and brown sugar; bring to a simmer and cook 4 minutes.
Add bell pepper, zucchini, and snap peas; cook 3 minutes until just tender.
Add shrimp and cook 3 to 4 minutes until pink and curled.
Remove from heat and stir in lime juice and Thai basil leaves.
Serve over jasmine rice.

title: Creamy Sun-Dried Tomato Spinach Penne
ingredients: 1/2 cup oil-packed sun-dried tomatoes, drained and sliced
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Cook penne in well-salted boiling water until al dente; reserve 1/2 cup pasta cooking water before draining.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and add garlic and sun-dried tomatoes.
Cook 2 minutes, stirring, until garlic is golden and fragrant.
Pour in heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer; cook 3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in Parmesan, Italian seasoning, and red pepper flakes until cheese is melted.
Add spinach and toss until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Add drained pasta and toss to coat, adding reserved pasta water a splash at a time to loosen the sauce.
Season with salt and pepper and garnish with fresh basil.

title: Slow-Roasted Harissa Lamb Shoulder
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C).
Combine harissa, garlic, olive oil, cumin, coriander, paprika, lemon zest, salt, and pepper into a thick paste.
Score lamb shoulder deeply in several places and rub the paste all over and into the cuts.
Heat a Dutch oven over high heat, add the lamb, and sear on all sides until browned, about 8 minutes total.
Remove lamb and reduce heat to medium; sauté onion in the same pot for 3 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes, broth, and lemon juice; stir to combine and scrape up any browned bits.
Return lamb to the pot, cover tightly, and roast in the oven for 3.5 hours until meat is falling from the bone.
Shred meat with two forks, stir into pan juices, and serve garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Miso Glazed Eggplant and Tofu Broil
ingredients: 14 oz firm tofu, pressed and sliced into 1-inch planks
2 cups steamed white rice for serving
steps: Preheat broiler to high and line a large baking sheet with foil.
Whisk together miso paste, mirin, tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and ginger in a small bowl until smooth.
Score eggplant flesh in a shallow crosshatch pattern and brush cut sides with vegetable oil.
Arrange eggplant cut-side up and tofu planks in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet.
Brush the miso glaze generously over both the eggplant and tofu.
Broil 10 to 12 minutes, watching closely, until eggplant is tender and glaze is caramelized and bubbling.
Serve over steamed rice and garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

title: Chipotle Chicken Tinga Stuffed Sweet Potatoes
ingredients: 2 chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, minced
1 tbsp adobo sauce from the can
Fresh cilantro and lime wedges for garnish
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C), pierce sweet potatoes all over with a fork, and bake directly on the oven rack for 45 to 55 minutes until tender.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and add onion; cook 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, oregano, and cumin; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chicken breasts, fire-roasted tomatoes, chipotle peppers, and adobo sauce; stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 22 to 25 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Shred chicken with two forks directly in the skillet and stir into the sauce; cook 2 more minutes uncovered.
Split sweet potatoes open and press sides to open them wide.
Fill each sweet potato generously with chicken tinga and top with sliced avocado, fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.

title: Indian Saag Paneer with Basmati Rice
ingredients: 2 cups basmati rice, cooked for serving
steps: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 1 minute, drain well, and blend to a smooth purée; set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and fry paneer cubes until golden on all sides, about 4 minutes; remove and set aside.
Add remaining ghee to the pan, add cumin seeds, and let sizzle for 30 seconds.
Add onion and cook over medium heat, stirring often, for 7 minutes until deep golden brown.
Stir in garlic and ginger; cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes, garam masala, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne; cook 5 minutes until the mixture darkens and oil separates slightly.
Pour in spinach purée and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in heavy cream and fried paneer; simmer 3 minutes until heated through and sauce is creamy.
Season with salt and serve over basmati rice.

title: Greek Lemon Roasted Chicken with Root Vegetables
ingredients: 1 large red onion, cut into wedges
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, oregano, thyme, smoked paprika, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper in a bowl.
Spread carrots, parsnips, fennel, onion, and garlic cloves in a single layer in a large roasting pan.
Drizzle half of the marinade over the vegetables and toss to coat evenly.
Nestle chicken leg quarters skin-side up on top of the vegetables.
Pour the remaining marinade over the chicken and season tops with extra salt and pepper.
Roast 50 to 55 minutes until chicken skin is deeply golden and an instant-read thermometer reads 165°F in the thickest part.
Rest 5 minutes, then scatter fresh parsley over the pan and serve directly from the roasting dish.
